neighborhoods.name: Chianti Classico
neighborhoods.address: Chianti Classico, between Florence and Siena, 50022 Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.description: The original Chianti zone between Florence and Siena, defined by the Gallo Nero (Black Rooster) seal. Sangiovese-dominant reds from galestro and alberese soils with three tiers: annata, Riserva and Gran Selezione.
neighborhoods.vibe: Cypress-lined ridgelines, stone borghi and Sangiovese vineyards from Greve to Castelnuovo Berardenga; the historic heart of Tuscan wine.

verified.address_quoted: Chianti Classico, between Florence and Siena, Tuscany, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Brunello di Montalcino
neighborhoods.address: Montalcino, 53024 Province of Siena, Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.description: The hilltop commune south of Siena whose 100% Sangiovese Grosso clone reaches Italy's most coveted expression. Minimum five years of ageing (six for Riserva) including two in oak before release.
neighborhoods.vibe: Sun-baked Sangiovese Grosso vineyards around a fortified hilltop town; austere, age-worthy reds that define Italian wine prestige.

verified.address_quoted: Montalcino, 53024 Province of Siena, Tuscany, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano
neighborhoods.address: Montepulciano, 53045 Province of Siena, Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.description: Sangiovese (locally called Prugnolo Gentile) from the steep terraces around the Renaissance hilltop town of Montepulciano. Minimum 70% Sangiovese; two years ageing, three for Riserva. The 2025 Pieve sub-zoning project added 12 village-level crus.
neighborhoods.vibe: Renaissance hilltop town surrounded by Prugnolo Gentile terraces; structured Sangiovese with the 2025 Pieve cru classification reshaping the appellation.

neighborhoods.name: Bolgheri
neighborhoods.address: Bolgheri, 57022 Castagneto Carducci, Livorno, Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.classification: Bolgheri DOC and Bolgheri Sassicaia DOC
neighborhoods.description: Coastal Tyrrhenian appellation south of Livorno, built on Bordeaux varieties since the 1944 plantings at Tenuta San Guido. Bolgheri DOC permits Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Syrah and Sangiovese; Sassicaia has its own single-estate DOC.
neighborhoods.vibe: Cypress avenue, sea-tempered gravels and Bordeaux-cloned reds; the Tyrrhenian terroir that defined the Super Tuscan category.

neighborhoods.name: Carmignano
neighborhoods.address: Carmignano, 59015 Province of Prato, Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.description: Small DOCG west of Florence with documented Cabernet plantings since the 16th century when Caterina de' Medici imported French vines. Minimum 50% Sangiovese plus 10 to 20% Cabernet Sauvignon or Cabernet Franc.
neighborhoods.vibe: Compact DOCG west of Florence with Medici-era Cabernet history; quietly proto-Super-Tuscan and led by Capezzana.

neighborhoods.name: Vernaccia di San Gimignano
neighborhoods.address: San Gimignano, 53037 Province of Siena, Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.description: Tuscany's flagship white DOCG and the first Italian DOC (1966) before its 1993 DOCG elevation. Minimum 85% Vernaccia grown on the sandstone slopes around the medieval tower town of San Gimignano.
neighborhoods.vibe: Stone towers, sandstone slopes and a citrus-driven white that was Italy's first DOC; the only DOCG white in central Tuscany.

neighborhoods.name: Maremma
neighborhoods.address: Maremma, 58100 Province of Grosseto, Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.classification: Maremma Toscana DOC and Morellino di Scansano DOCG
neighborhoods.description: The coastal plain and hill country of southern Tuscany covering Grosseto province. Maremma Toscana DOC was created in 2011 as a broad varietal-friendly umbrella; Morellino di Scansano DOCG inside it focuses on Sangiovese.
neighborhoods.vibe: Wide coastal plains, butteri cowboys and emerging modern estates; the most flexible varietal palette in Tuscany.

neighborhoods.name: Chianti Rufina
neighborhoods.address: Rufina, 50068 Province of Florence, Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.description: The smallest of the seven Chianti DOCG sub-zones, set north-east of Florence in the Sieve valley at higher elevation. Cooler microclimate produces more aromatic, longer-lived Sangiovese; Frescobaldi's Nipozzano estate is the historic anchor.
neighborhoods.vibe: High-elevation valley north-east of Florence; the most age-worthy Chianti sub-zone outside the Classico heartland.

neighborhoods.name: Chianti Colli Senesi
neighborhoods.address: Colli Senesi, 53100 Province of Siena, Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.classification: Chianti Colli Senesi DOCG (Chianti DOCG sub-zone)
neighborhoods.description: The largest Chianti DOCG sub-zone by area, spread across the Sienese hills outside the Classico boundary including the slopes around Montalcino, Montepulciano and San Gimignano. Sangiovese-led with the most varied terroir of any Chianti sub-zone.
neighborhoods.vibe: The widest-ranging Chianti sub-zone across the Sienese hills outside Classico; everyday Sangiovese with a price-quality tilt.

neighborhoods.name: Chianti Colli Fiorentini
neighborhoods.address: Colli Fiorentini, 50019 Province of Florence, Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.classification: Chianti Colli Fiorentini DOCG (Chianti DOCG sub-zone)
neighborhoods.description: Chianti DOCG sub-zone in the hills around Florence outside the Classico boundary, stretching from Bagno a Ripoli south through San Casciano and Tavarnelle. Sangiovese with a softer, earlier-drinking profile than Rufina or Classico.
sub_communes: San Casciano in Val di Pesa
neighborhoods.vibe: The Florentine hills ring of Chianti DOCG; approachable, dinner-table Sangiovese close to the city.

neighborhoods.name: Morellino di Scansano
neighborhoods.address: Scansano, 58054 Province of Grosseto, Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.description: DOCG since 2007 within the Maremma. Minimum 85% Sangiovese (locally called Morellino) on warmer, lower-elevation hills than the Sangiovese benchmarks further north. Riserva requires two years of ageing.
neighborhoods.vibe: Maremma's flagship DOCG; warmer, fleshier Sangiovese (locally Morellino) within sight of the Tyrrhenian coast.

neighborhoods.name: Val d'Orcia
neighborhoods.address: Val d'Orcia, 53027 Province of Siena, Tuscany, Italy
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO World Heritage landscape between Montalcino and Vino Nobile zones. Orcia DOC (2000) covers Sangiovese-led reds and a small white production from the iconic rolling hills that frame Pienza, Montalcino and Castiglione d'Orcia.
neighborhoods.vibe: The postcard valley between Brunello and Vino Nobile; UNESCO-listed and slowly building its own Sangiovese DOC.
